items.title: ANU approved $250m cost-cutting plan without clear evidence, report finds

children.content: Australian National University's leadership approved a controversial $250 million cost-cutting program without a clear understanding of the problem it was trying to solve or the risks involved, a report has found.

image.altText: Signage for the ANU.

items.title: Rebecca Vassarotti returns to ACT politics after Shane Rattenbury's exit

children.content: Rebecca Vassarotti has been re-elected to the ACT Legislative Assembly in an electoral countback after the resignation of ACT Greens leader Shane Rattenbury.

image.altText: A woman with short brown hair wearing a green jacket smiles, standing behind microphones.

items.title: Canberra Liberals say alleged violent threat was a 'misunderstanding'

children.content: The Canberra Liberals have distanced themselves from former leader Leanne Castley's allegation that she was threatened with physical assault, saying the remark related to an "animated discussion" that was quickly resolved.

gallery.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

thumbnail.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

items.title: 80yo to plead guilty over Canberra car crash that killed 4yo boy

children.content: Police allege the woman was driving a blue hatchback when it hit the child before continuing into a shopfront at the Southlands Shopping Centre in Mawson in January this year.

image.altText: A car crashed into a shopping centre with police tape.

items.title: These are the 'worst offenders' in Australian packaging

children.content: A trendy plastic-and-metal takeaway cup used by cafes for iced drinks has been named Australia's worst packaging in the first Unpackit Awards.

image.altText: Four dirty, empty can-shaped plastic drinking vessels with a metal pull tab top.

items.title: Leanne Castley is already partyless but some Liberals want her gone entirely

children.content: Whether they're being driven away or choosing to jump in a getaway car, the women who have recently led the Canberra Liberals are no longer anywhere near the driver's seat.

gallery.altText: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer looks serious.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer looks serious.

items.title: Court hears 'ISIS bride' mistreated a woman her family enslaved

children.content: Prosecutors have argued Zeinab Ahmad actively participated in the mistreatment of a woman her family enslaved in Syria.

image.altText: A court sketch of Zeinab Ahmad

items.title: Elderly NSW couple stabbed, alleged intruder shot during home invasion

children.content: Two people in their 70s have been critically injured and an alleged intruder has been shot during an alleged home invasion in northern NSW this morning.

image.altText: Two elderly people smiling

items.title: Australian arrested for alleged cannabis vape liquid in Indonesia

children.content: The woman reportedly told officers she used the cannabis liquid to treat knee pain and depression, but authorities say she faces up to 20 years in jail and a hefty fine.

image.altText: A woman with a pixelated face stands in front of a wall.

items.title: Motorcyclist says he owes his life to bystanders who lifted car off him

children.content: Tyler Weibe was on his way to work when the crash occurred on Brisbane's north side, leaving him with broken ribs, a broken collarbone and a collapsed lung.

image.altText: A group of people on a road lifting a car off a motorcyclist after a crash.

gallery.altText: A group of people on a road lifting a car off a motorcyclist after a crash.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people on a road lifting a car off a motorcyclist after a crash.

avatar.alt: A young woman with blonde hair, smiling at the camera.

items.title: Souths stung by 'sticky spray' ruling as Manly continue Foran resurgence

children.content: The Manly Sea Eagles have provisionally moved into the NRL top four with a 28-14 win over the South Sydney Rabbitohs at Brookvale Oval.

image.altText: Referee Adam Gee talks to Cameron Murray of the Rabbitohs

items.title: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash

children.content: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.

items.title: Teen's brutal death a flashpoint over violence against women and femicide

children.content: The alleged brutal murder of 14-year-old Agostina Vega in Argentina has reignited a generation-defining movement demanding justice for the women victims of crime and violence.

gallery.altText: A dark-haired female protester holding placards with the greyscale face of a teenage girl with the words "Justice For Agostina".

thumbnail.altText: A dark-haired female protester holding placards with the greyscale face of a teenage girl with the words "Justice For Agostina".

items.title: Netizens in China hunt for access to open internet as VPNs drop out

children.content: People in China say it is increasingly difficult to access the open internet, but they continue to find ways around Beijing's censorship apparatus.

image.altText: A graphic of an Asian girl using her mobile phone with some social media icons floating next to her.

items.title: Israel and Lebanon renew ceasefire but Hezbollah not present at talks

children.content: Lebanon and Israel have agreed to a ceasefire, according to a joint statement with the United States.

image.altText: Smoke billows over buildings in southern Lebanon after a missile strike

items.title: What happens when a Restoration Australia project takes five years

children.content: A planned 12-month build that's still underway three years later is the running joke of design shows. But what happens when you're a TV producer trying to make a series of it?

gallery.altText: A camera films an old building with scaffolding around it.
Anthony Burke wears a suit, smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A camera films an old building with scaffolding around it.
Anthony Burke wears a suit, smiling.

items.title: Who gets a public holiday on Monday?

children.content: Many Australians are about to have a long weekend thanks to the King's Birthday public holiday on Monday. But not everyone will have the day off.

image.altText: A close up of King Charles wearing a grey suit with a dark pink tie

items.title: How to move forward from estrangement

children.content: Experts say healing might involve reducing emotional distress and gaining clarity from the situation.

image.altText: Two people sitting on a park bench with their backs to the camera

items.title: What's got the Pope and most Australians worried? This hyped new AI technology

children.content: Most Australians appear to distrust and dislike artificial intelligence despite using technology more than people in other countries.

articles.title: Australia joins exclusive club granting access to advanced AI

children.content: Fresh access to AI deemed too dangerous for public release has ramped up pressure on the government to respond to possible risks.

image.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

articles.title: Economic report says 82 occupations have been 'AI-disrupted'

children.content: A new economic report by consultancy firm Deloitte says the AI "winners" will be organisations that combine human and machine strengths, with recruiters saying human judgement will still be key.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt and long hair looks at camera.

gallery.altText: A close up of Pope Leo.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of Pope Leo.

items.title: Unlicensed chip factory lands WA's 'spud king' in hot water

children.content: Potato baron Tony Galati's business group has been fined $20,000 after it was found to have built an illegal Spuddies potato chip factory in WA's South West.

image.altText: A supermarket owner in his store.

items.title: Neo-Nazi hecklers found guilty over Anzac Day booing

children.content: Three white supremacists who loudly booed during a Welcome to Country ceremony at an Anzac Day dawn service in Melbourne have been found guilty of offensive behaviour and ordered to pay fines.

image.altText: A man in a blue suit and white shirt carrying a book walks outside a court room.

items.title: Pat Cummins hoping to stay fit for 'unprecedented' 21-Test stretch

children.content: Australian men's captain Pat Cummins says his body is feeling "awesome" ahead of a hectic 12-month stretch of Test cricket.

gallery.altText: Australia bowler Pat Cummins on his knees celebrates a wicket.

thumbnail.altText: Australia bowler Pat Cummins on his knees celebrates a wicket.

items.title: Chinese spies using LinkedIn to steal secrets from recruits, Five Eyes warns

children.content: Australia and its partners in the Five Eyes intelligence alliance warn that Chinese spies are using job platforms to post fake ads to lure recruits with access to sensitive information.

image.altText: A screenshot of several apps on a screen, including LinkedIn.

items.title: MP uses parliament to reveal threats to NZYQ deportees in Nauru

children.content: A cohort of former detainees deported to Nauru by the federal government have faced threats of violence and verbal abuse, independent MP Andrew Wilkie has revealed under parliamentary privilege.

image.altText: Andrew Wilkie close

items.title: White Australia's bid to remove hate listing dismissed

children.content: The High Court has dismissed an application by the group White Australia which had hoped to lift its listing as a hate group ahead of a formal High Court hearing later in the year.

image.altText: The front of a courthouse

items.title: Boxing trainer suspended after neo-Nazi Sewell trained at his gym

children.content: ABC NEWS Verify can reveal that neo-Nazi Thomas Sewell has been training at a boxing gym in the outer-Melbourne suburb of Diamond Creek, a short drive from where he is living. The gym has been suspended by the sport's governing body.

image.altText: A bald man with a moustache in the foreground superimposed over a blurred boxing ring

items.title: The streaming platform exploding in popularity

children.content: Whether or not you've heard of the livestreaming platform Kick, there’s a good chance you’ll have seen clips from it.
Kick has become home to some of the internet's most controversial creators — from celebrities and manosphere influencers to looksmaxxers.
But what is Kick, and why has it become such a major player in livestreaming?

image.altText: A person in a dark shirt gestures while speaking against a soft purple backdrop. Bold text reads, "WHAT'S THE APPEAL OF KICK?"

gallery.altText: A person in a dark shirt gestures while speaking against a soft purple backdrop. Bold text reads, "WHAT'S THE APPEAL OF KICK?"

thumbnail.altText: A person in a dark shirt gestures while speaking against a soft purple backdrop. Bold text reads, "WHAT'S THE APPEAL OF KICK?"

items.title: US House votes to rein in Trump on Iran war

children.content: The US House of Representatives delivers President Donald Trump a rare rebuke, voting to halt military action in his war with Iran.

image.altText: Donald Trump gestures with both hands as he speaks from the Resolute Desk.

items.title: Far South Coast breaks records in oyster growing turnaround

children.content: While oyster growers have had a tough time recently, one lake on the NSW Far South Coast is bucking the trend and setting new records along the way.

image.altText: Oyster farmer in an estaury.

items.title: Meta hits out at Labor's plan to make tech giants pay for news

children.content: Tech giant Meta criticises the Australian government's plan to make social media companies pay for news, calling it a "grossly unfair" and "discriminatory tax".

image.altText: Three hands in silhouette in front of Meta logo.

items.title: Fewer cars stolen across Australia — except in Victoria

children.content: Victoria has higher vehicle insurance claims over stolen cars than all other mainland states combined, new data from the Insurance Council of Australia reveals.

image.altText: Attempted Tallangatta car theft

items.title: Australia's economic slowdown is just beginning, experts warn

children.content: The economy grew just 0.3 per cent in the first quarter, gross domestic product per capita went backwards, and interest rate rises and cost-of-living pressures are weighing on households.
